traits.name: The Garden Is the Monster
traits.desc: The garden's body is the entire grove — the walking trees, the vines, and the pool at its heart, which is not water at all but the garden's stomach: a basin of clear digestive fluid held in its own flesh. Its speed is 0, but its growth is everywhere: it can make attacks from any tree, root, or vine within the garden. It dies when its hit points are gone; the pool clouds, sinks away, and leaves a bare basin of grey flesh.

traits.name: The Garden Closes
traits.desc: At the start of each of the garden's turns, the ring of walking trunks and laced vines advances 10 feet inward, shrinking the open floor. Ground inside the ring is difficult terrain as roots surface. A creature that ends its turn within 5 feet of the advancing wall must succeed on a DC 14 Strength saving throw or be grappled by it (escape DC 14) and pulled 10 feet toward the pool. The archway out seals fully 3 rounds after the garden closes — until then a creature can squeeze through with 10 feet of movement.

traits.name: Fire-Shy
traits.desc: When the garden takes fire damage, it recoils: the closing wall does not advance at the start of its next turn, and until the end of that turn every creature it is grappling has advantage on checks made to escape.

traits.name: The Pool Is the Stomach
traits.desc: The pool is not water — it only looks like it, right up until you're in it. A creature in the pool is wrapped and held (Restrained; escape DC 14 Strength (Athletics) check), and at the start of each of its turns feeding spines rise out of the fluid and pierce it: 18 (4d8) Acid damage as the digestive fluid runs into the wounds. A creature reduced to 0 hit points in the pool is pulled under and dissolved.

traits.name: False Chorus
traits.desc: The garden imitates birdsong, warmth, and stillness. A creature that listens closely and succeeds on a DC 15 Wisdom (Perception) check notices the song is a loop — the same calls from the same places. When the garden is below half its hit points, the chorus restarts on its own, sped up and wrong: each creature that can hear it must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or have disadvantage on its next attack roll as the sound gets inside its head.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The garden makes two Grasping Bough attacks. Grappling creatures does not limit how many attacks it can make — it has no shortage of limbs.

actions.name: Grasping Bough
actions.desc: Melee Attack Roll: +7, reach 20 ft. 13 (2d8 + 4) Bludgeoning damage, and if the target is a Large or smaller creature it is wrapped in coils of vine and root (Grappled, escape DC 14). A single attack that deals 10 or more Slashing damage to a grappling bough severs it, freeing the creature it holds — the damage still counts against the garden's hit points.

actions.name: Reel
actions.desc: Each creature grappled by the garden is pulled 15 feet toward the pool.

traits.name: The Wall Provides
traits.desc: Five feed-pipes rise from the floor around the chamber (AC 15, HP 20 each, immune to poison and psychic damage). At the start of the necromancer's turn, while the wall of flesh stands, he regains 3 hit points per intact pipe (15 while all five stand). Destroying pipes is the party's lever — make it visibly work.

traits.name: Given, Not Taken
traits.desc: Damage the necromancer deals to himself cannot be prevented, reduced, or redirected by any means.

traits.name: Deeper Giving
traits.desc: While the necromancer has fewer than half his hit points, his attacks deal an extra 4 (1d8) Necrotic damage. The more he gives, the more he has to give.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The necromancer makes two Tithe attacks, or one Tithe attack and one Giving Edge attack.

actions.name: Tithe
actions.desc: Ranged Spell Attack: +7, range 60 ft. 14 (4d6) Necrotic damage, and the target can't regain hit points until the start of the necromancer's next turn.

actions.name: Giving Edge
actions.desc: Melee Attack Roll: +4 with the small clean knife, reach 5 ft. 3 (1d4 + 1) Slashing damage plus 9 (2d8) Necrotic damage.

actions.name: Given Flesh
actions.desc: The necromancer opens his own arm, taking 25 damage (this damage cannot be prevented or reduced), and a Flesh Offering rises from a blood-channel within 30 feet of him. It acts immediately after his turn. He can sustain no more than two offerings at once, and he will do this even when it might kill him — especially then.

actions.name: Circle of Gift (Recharge 5–6)
actions.desc: Blood erupts from every channel in the floor. Each creature standing on the chamber floor must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking 21 (6d6) Necrotic damage and having the Restrained condition until the end of its next turn as clutching hands rise from the channels on a failed save, or half as much damage only on a successful one.

actions.name: Step Through the Given
actions.desc: The necromancer sinks into a blood-channel and rises from another point on the lattice within 30 feet of where he sank.

actions.name: Offered Shield
actions.desc: When the necromancer is hit by an attack while a Flesh Offering is within 5 feet of him, the offering takes the damage instead. It steps in front of the blow. It was given for exactly this.

traits.name: Still Giving
traits.desc: If damage reduces the offering to 0 hit points, it must make a Constitution saving throw with a DC of 5 plus the damage taken, unless the damage is radiant or from a critical hit. On a success, the offering drops to 1 hit point instead.

traits.name: Bear Down
traits.desc: A creature grappled by the offering takes 7 (2d6) Bludgeoning damage at the start of each of the offering's turns.

traits.name: Slow and Certain
traits.desc: The offering can't take reactions, and it always moves toward the nearest creature it can sense unless the necromancer directs it otherwise.

traits.name: Given Back
traits.desc: When the offering is destroyed, it loses cohesion and drains away into the nearest blood-channel. Nothing is wasted.

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The offering makes two Slam attacks. It can grapple one creature with each of its arms.

actions.name: Slam
actions.desc: Melee Attack Roll: +6, reach 10 ft. 11 (2d6 + 4) Bludgeoning damage, and if the target is a Medium or smaller creature it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 14).
